chapter_title.en: Chapter on Marking and Garlanding (sacrificial animals)

text: 2575 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «تُقَلِّدُهَا نَعْلاً خَلَقاً قَدْ صَلَّيْتَ فِيهَا وَ اَلْإِشْعَارُ وَ اَلتَّقْلِيدُ بِمَنْزِلَةِ اَلتَّلْبِيَةِ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2575 - In a narration from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
"You garland it with an old sandal that you have prayed in, and both marking (Ish'ar) and garlanding (Taqleed) hold the same status as the Talbiyah."
